commit.message:
Add nightly release workflow (#5)

* Add nightly release workflow

Builds all 6 platform binaries from main daily at 04:00 UTC and
publishes them to a rolling 'nightly' GitHub prerelease. Also
supports manual triggering via workflow_dispatch.

- Skips the build entirely if main hasn't changed since the last run
  (detected via GitHub API — no checkout needed on skip days)
- Version string: <pom-version>-nightly+<short-sha>
- Rolling strategy: single 'nightly' tag and release, updated each run
- Tag is moved only after the release is successfully published

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Address PR #5 review comments

- Add concurrency group to prevent overlapping scheduled/manual runs
- Hardcode 'main' as default branch (removes unreliable event context lookup)
- Mirror release.yml nullglob + empty-check pattern in artifact collection
- Use <<-EOF heredoc to strip leading tabs from release notes

commit.message:
Task 28: Add max-level ambient particle effect

When the player reaches level 5 (full knight armor), a subtle ambient
particle effect activates — small glowing sparks drift off the character.

Implementation (in main.cpp):
- LevelParticle struct with position, velocity, lifetime, size, color
- Spawns ~3 particles/second at random offsets within player bounding box
- Particles drift upward with slight horizontal drift, fade from 0.8
  alpha to transparent over 1-2 seconds
- Three randomized colors: warm gold, white-hot, ember orange
- Rendered as small circles via drawCircle() in world space (before
  endFrame), benefiting from the lighting system
- Automatically disabled below level 5, re-enabled on game load at max level

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: eliminate duplicate diagnostics from manual validation

Bug #1 (HIGH): Remove connection.sendDiagnostics() from the
ditacraft/validateFile handler — the client already applies diagnostics
from the response into the 'ditacraft-manual' DiagnosticCollection.
Pushing via sendDiagnostics created a second identical set in the LSP
collection, doubling every entry in the Problems panel.

Bug #2 (MEDIUM): Register onDidSaveTextDocument and onDidCloseTextDocument
handlers in initializeValidator to clear stale manual diagnostics. Without
this, manual validation entries persisted alongside auto-validation
diagnostics after file save.

Bug #3 (LOW): Fix incorrect version reference in comment (v0.8.0 → v0.7.0).

* fix: replace range-keyed pnpm overrides with plain keys; add .trivyignore for residual false positives

Trivy v0.69.3 misreads range-based pnpm override *keys* (e.g. `hono@<4.12.12`)
as installed package versions, creating new false-positive Code Scanning alerts
that differ from the base-branch fingerprints and cause the Trivy PR check to fail.

Fix:
- Convert five safe overrides to plain keys so Trivy reads the *patched* version
  directly: vite→7.3.2, hono→4.12.12, @hono/node-server→1.19.13, lodash→4.18.1,
  serialize-javascript→7.0.5
- Keep range-scoped keys for undici (avoids overriding @sentry/cli's undici@6.x),
  picomatch (2.x and 4.x sibling installs) and path-to-regexp (0.x/6.x/8.x)
- Add .trivyignore to suppress the residual false-positive CVE IDs that Trivy
  reports from those three necessary range keys
- Bump direct hono dep to ^4.12.12 in root and frontend package.json
- Regenerate pnpm-lock.yaml

commit.message:
fix: clear stale prefetched_list and validate index bounds on advance

Root cause: _prefetched_list was set by an early prefetch (e.g. queue
2→7 items) and never cleared when subsequent tracks advanced via branch
2a (direct advance). This blocked future prefetches and left a stale
list. When the last track ended (index 6/7), _signal_track_completion
used the stale 7-item list with next_index=7 → Ynison error 400030001.

Fixes:
- Clear _prefetched_list on direct advance (branch 2a) so _maybe_prefetch
  can re-trigger for the new queue tail
- Validate next_index < len(expanded) before sending to Ynison
- If expanded list is too short, re-fetch before giving up

feat(project-switch): add ProjectContextRebinder with rollback semantics (#135)

* feat(project-switch): add ProjectContextRebinder with rollback semantics

Implements P3-06 (Issue #134): Project Context Rebinding on Switch.

- Port interfaces: KgTrieCachePort, EpisodicMemoryCachePort, RebindableService
- Single composite lifecycle participant ('context-rebinder') registered with
  ProjectLifecycle, coordinating all context subsystem rebindings
- Rollback-on-bind-failure: on partial bind, unbind successfully-bound services
  from failed project (reverse order) then re-bind ALL to previous project
- 25 tests covering registration, unbind/bind ordering, rollback, optional
  services, async, tracking, logging, and edge cases
- LoggerPort exported for type-safe test mocking

* fix: guard lastBoundProjectId update against mid-loop signal abort

  to prevent partial bind from corrupting rollback target (INV-10)
- Add mid-loop abort test using getter-backed signal to cover the code path
  that was previously untested (pre-aborted signal only hit early-return)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: cleanup partially-bound services on initial bind failure

When bind fails and there is no previous project (initial bind), the
successfully-bound services were left dangling. Now cleanupPartialBind()
unbinds them in reverse order, preventing inconsistent partial state.

Addresses Duck R2 finding #2.

Duck R2 findings #1 (multi-scope rollback) and #3 (integration test with
real lifecycle timeout) are dismissed as out-of-scope for P3-06:
- #1: ProjectLifecycle multi-scope is infrastructure-level; no current code
  exercises concurrent multi-scope switching
- #3: Unit tests cover abort logic; integration tests are beyond this PR

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: abort path cleanup + orphaned JSDoc + scope constraint doc

R3 audit findings (Duck + Sonnet):

1. BLOCKING (Duck): When signal.aborted fires mid-loop during bind,
   the break exits without cleaning up partially-bound services. Now
   triggers rollback (if previous project) or cleanupPartialBind (if
   initial bind) — same recovery as the catch path.

2. NIT (Sonnet): Orphaned JSDoc block for rollback() was separated
   from its target function by the cleanupPartialBind insertion.
   Moved JSDoc to directly precede rollback().

3. NON-BLOCKING (Duck): Added scope constraint documentation in
   module header explaining single-scope assumption and future
   extension path for multi-scope.

Tests: 28 (was 26) — added abort-with-rollback and abort-with-cleanup
scenarios.

* 🔖 Auto-update version to v2.1.106

* fix: format package.json and update version in index.html

* Modularizes game logic and adds automated test coverage

Separates core game logic into a reusable engine module to enable isolated unit testing and future UI migrations. Integrates Vitest for automated testing, achieving initial coverage of key mechanics and serialization. Updates scripts and dependencies to support modern JS modules and test workflows. Lays groundwork for robust, maintainable development and facilitates eventual Angular migration as outlined in the new implementation plan.

* Remove debug logs and update dev instructions

Cleans up code by removing numerous debug console statements related to dungeon and control logic, resulting in cleaner output and improved maintainability. Updates the documentation to include clear instructions for installing dependencies, running tests, and generating coverage reports, making development and testing workflows more accessible.

* .

* feat: add .gitignore and refactor game state management in game.js

* refactor: remove coverage report, add coffee production logic, and update tests

- Deleted the coverage report file as it is no longer needed.
- Introduced a new function `produceCoffee` in `game-engine.js` to handle coffee production logic.
- Updated `game.js` to utilize the new `produceCoffee` function and refactored validation logic.
- Removed the old `validateGameValues` function and replaced its calls with a new validation approach.
- Enhanced tests in `game-engine.test.js` to cover the new coffee production functionality and ensure state persistence after upgrades.

* feat: initialize Angular application with core structure and game engine

- Added package.json for Angular app setup with dependencies and scripts.
- Included favicon.ico for application branding.
- Created app configuration file for global error handling.
- Developed main application component with HTML and CSS.
- Implemented game engine service and logic for managing game state.
- Defined game state interfaces and initial values for upgrades and bosses.
- Set up TypeScript configuration files for application and testing.
- Added index.html as the entry point for the Angular application.
- Updated game.js to include bosses in the engine state.

* feat: add new game panels and enhance save/load functionality with state management

* feat: add GitHub Actions workflow for CI and automated deployment to GitHub Pages

* chore: remove outdated implementation plan document

* feat: implement GameStateService for state management and update GameEngineService to utilize it

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Fix autotuner worker device affinity (#104)

* Fix autotuner worker OOM by restricting workers to parent's CUDA device

In multi-rank training, autotuner subprocess workers all initialize a CUDA
context on device 0 (the default). When many ranks on the same node spawn
workers simultaneously, the CUDA context overhead on GPU 0 causes OOM.

Fix by setting CUDA_VISIBLE_DEVICES in the worker environment to the parent
rank's current device. The logical device index is mapped through the parent's
CUDA_VISIBLE_DEVICES to handle GPU UUIDs and MIG IDs.

We guard with torch.cuda.is_initialized() to avoid eagerly initializing CUDA
and to handle the case where autotuning runs before device selection.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Refactor: extract _get_current_cuda_device() helper

Move the logic for resolving the current physical CUDA device identifier
into a standalone function that returns None when the device cannot be
determined.
